title: CS2 Case Odds Calculator — Knife & Covert Drop Chances | CSDB.gg
description: Calculate your real odds of unboxing a knife, covert, or any rarity from CS2 cases. See expected costs, ROI analysis, and probability tables for every rarity tier.
heading: CS2 Case Odds Calculator
intro: Every CS2 case opening is a gamble. Use this calculator to understand exactly what your odds are before spending money on keys — and decide whether the expected return justifies the cost.

faqs.question: What are the odds of getting a knife from a CS2 case?
faqs.answer: The chance of unboxing a knife or glove (Rare Special Item) from any CS2 case is approximately 0.26%, or roughly 1 in 385 cases. This means you would statistically need to open 385 cases to expect one knife drop.

faqs.question: Are CS2 case odds the same for every case?
faqs.answer: Yes, all standard CS2 weapon cases share the same rarity drop rates. The odds of getting a Mil-Spec, Restricted, Classified, Covert, or Rare Special Item are consistent regardless of which case you open.

faqs.question: Is opening CS2 cases profitable?
faqs.answer: On average, no. The expected value of opening a standard case is typically $0.80–$1.50, while each case costs $2.49 plus a key. However, rare knife or glove drops can be worth hundreds or thousands of dollars, which is what makes it exciting.

faqs.question: How many cases do I need to open for a guaranteed knife?
faqs.answer: There is no guaranteed number — each case is an independent event. However, after opening 1,000 cases, you have roughly a 92.6% chance of having received at least one knife. After 1,500 cases, it rises to 98%.

faqs.question: What is the rarest item you can get from a CS2 case?
faqs.answer: The rarest items are StatTrak Factory New knives or gloves from the Rare Special Items pool. The combined probability of a specific StatTrak FN knife pattern can be as low as 1 in 100,000+ cases.
